title: Right to Equality
summary: The State shall not deny to any person equality before the law or the equal protection of the laws within the territory of India.

title: Protection of certain rights regarding freedom
summary: Guarantees freedoms including speech and expression, peaceful assembly, forming associations, free movement, residence, and practising any profession — subject to reasonable restrictions.

title: Protection in respect of conviction for offences
summary: No person can be punished under a law that did not exist when the act was done (no ex-post-facto law), no one can be prosecuted twice for the same offence (double jeopardy), and no one can be compelled to be a witness against themselves.

title: Right to Life and Personal Liberty
summary: No person shall be deprived of their life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law. Interpreted to include the rights to privacy, dignity, a clean environment and livelihood.

title: Right to Education
summary: The State shall provide free and compulsory education to all children aged 6 to 14 years.

title: Protection against arrest and detention
summary: An arrested person must be told the grounds of arrest, allowed to consult a lawyer of their choice, and produced before a magistrate within 24 hours of arrest.

title: Right to Constitutional Remedies
summary: The right to move the Supreme Court directly to enforce fundamental rights. The Court can issue writs such as habeas corpus, mandamus, prohibition, quo warranto and certiorari.

title: Right to Property
summary: No person shall be deprived of their property save by authority of law.
